_comment: Tidal Access Model Configuration. Tidal curve parameters used by access window calculator. Harmonic constants and secondary port offsets are documented here for reference but are currently implemented in harmonic.py and secondary_port.py respectively.

secondary_port_offset._comment: Portsmouth to Langstone Harbour corrections. Applied by KHM parser and secondary_port.py (for harmonic predictions). Validated April 2026 against UKHO half-hourly data for both ports: observed HW height delta 0.0-0.1m (mean +0.05m), not +0.24m as previously assumed. LW heights effectively identical between ports.

tidal_curve._comment: Parameters for Langstone Harbour asymmetric tidal curve used to interpolate heights between HW/LW events. The Solent exhibits an extended HW stand on the ebb.
tidal_curve._notes: flood_duration_fraction + ebb_duration_fraction must equal 1.0. The stand occurs near HW on the ebb. stand_height_fraction is the proportion of HW height maintained during the stand period. Calibrated against UKHO half-hourly Langstone data (21-22 April 2026): stand duration ~40min at 98% of HW height, consistent across springs and neaps. Impact: ebb threshold crossings ~10-15min later on springs, ~20-30min later on neaps where HW is close to threshold.

harmonic_reference._comment: Harmonic constants are implemented in app/harmonic.py. Documented here for reference only. 19 constituents calibrated April 2026 against 388 Admiralty reference points (91 HW/LW + 7 spring HW + 288 half-hourly) spanning May-December 2026 plus April 2026 half-hourly data.
harmonic_reference.accuracy: Height RMS ~0.1m on unseen half-hourly data, ~0.4m on HW/LW peaks (dominated by Solent stand effect). HW/LW timing stdev ~17-19 min.
